_description: ESPN team/player data for Portugal and Spain across all 4 games played so far (3 group + R32 knockout)
espn_event_lookup: not yet played - no event id
key_findings: Bruno Fernandes: SOT = [0,1,1,1] across 4 games. He hits 1+ SOT reliably (3/4 = 75%) but NEVER 2+. Same drought pattern already validated in POR-CRO (2+ SOT NO won +27.17), but for THIS match's Q3 (1+ SOT), the signal points the opposite way: his floor is solid, market looks underpriced.
Cristiano Ronaldo: goals in 2/4 games (UZB blowout brace + CRO knockout goal). The CRO goal is the relevant precedent (competitive knockout match, not a blowout) - still productive, no drought.

Lamine Yamal: only 1 goal contribution in 4 games (vs weakest opponent, Saudi Arabia), ZERO assists all tournament. His 4-SOT game vs Austria was a shot-volume outlier against a very weak side, not a conversion signal. Real score/assist rate ~25%, well below market.

Diogo Costa saves: [1,2,6,5] across 4 games - clearly bimodal. Low-save games came against weak/overmatched opponents (Congo DR, Uzbekistan blowout); high-save games came against the two strongest, most possession-heavy opponents faced so far (Colombia away, Croatia). Spain today will be Portugal's toughest test yet (10 SOT vs Austria last game) - regime strongly favors the high end.

Substitution counts in regulation across the last 4 knockout/R32 games played by these exact 4 teams: POR-CRO=9, ESP-AUT=10, USA-BIH=8, BEL-SEN=8. Strong signal both teams use most/all of their 5 allowed subs in knockout football.
